meta.description: AUFK table
Customer cleared items – AR line items, amounts (DMBTR, WRBTR).
Accounting document segment – amounts, customers, vendors.
CEPC table
CKHS table
CKIS table
CKIT table
CKMLCR table
CKMLHD table
CKMLPP table
COEP table
COSP table
CRHD table
CSKS table
Purchase requisition (requisition items, materials, quantities).
Purchase order header – vendor (LIFNR), dates (BEDAT), currency.
Purchase order item – amounts (NETWR), quantities, materials.

New G/L line items (FAGLFLEXA) – general ledger actual postings. Key columns: prctr=profit center, racct=GL account, rbukrs=company code, hsl=amount in local currency, wsl=amount in transaction currency, ksl=controlling area currency, ryear=fiscal year, gjahr=fiscal year, poper=posting period, drcrk=debit/credit indicator (D/H), budat=posting date, belnr=document number, kokrs=controlling area, rcntr=cost center, cost_elem=cost element, segment=segment. Use for: GL totals by profit center, account, cost center.

columns.prctr: Profit center – use for GROUP BY when answering by profit center
columns.hsl: Amount in local (company code) currency – primary cost/amount column for aggregation
columns.msl: Quantity in base unit of measure
columns.gjahr: Fiscal year (same as ryear; use for date filters)

meta.description: KEKO table
KEPH table
Customer master – names, country (LAND1), industry (brsch). Use T016T for industry text.
Customer partners (payer, ship-to, bill-to relationships).
Customer sales data (sales area, pricing, related attributes).

Pricing conditions (KONV) – condition records for sales pricing, discounts, surcharges. Key columns: knumv=pricing document number (join VBRK.KNUMV or VBAK.KNUMV), kschl=condition type (PR00=standard price, K007=customer discount, RA01=rebate), kbetr=condition rate/amount, waers=currency, kawrt=condition base value, kappl=application (V=sales, M=purchasing), kposn=item number. Use for: price condition analysis, discount by material/customer group.

columns.knumv: Pricing document number – join key to VBRK.KNUMV or VBAK.KNUMV
columns.kschl: Condition type (e.g. PR00=standard price, K007=customer discount, RA01=rebate)
columns.kawrt: Condition base value (quantity or value used as basis)
columns.kbetr: Condition rate/amount – the price or discount value
columns.kpein: Condition pricing unit (e.g. per 1 or per 100)
columns.kstat: Condition is statistical (X=yes: does not affect price)

meta.description: Vendor master – vendor names, addresses.
Vendor company code (vendor accounting, payment terms).
Vendor purchasing org (vendor–purchasing org data).
Outbound delivery header – delivery documents, ship-to.
Outbound delivery item – products, quantities.
Document segment (document item data).
Material descriptions – MAKTX for product names. Join on MATNR.
Plant data for material (plant-level material attributes).
Units of measure for material (UOM conversion).
International Article Numbers (EAN/UPC) for materials.
Material document header (goods movement header, posting date).
Sales data for materials (sales org, distribution channel, pricing group).
Vendor invoice header – vendor (LIFNR), amount (RMWWR), date (BUDAT).
Reservation/dependent requirements (material reservations, requirements).
Vendor invoice item – amounts (WRBTR, DMBTR), quantities.
STKO table
STPO table
Industry descriptions – BRTXT for industry names. Join KNA1.brsch = T016T.brsch.
Sales order header – customer, order dates.
Sales order item – products, quantities, values.
Schedule lines for sales document items (delivery quantities and dates).

Sales document flow (VBFA) – traces document predecessor/successor chains (sales order → delivery → billing). Key columns: vbelv=predecessor document, vbeln=successor document, vbtyp_n=successor type (J=delivery, M=billing, C=order confirmation), posnv/posnn=predecessor/successor item, matnr=material. Use for: tracing order-to-invoice flow, finding all billing docs for a sales order.
Billing document header – invoice dates, customer (KUNAG), currency.
Same as VBRP – billing item for sales analysis.
